logs.name: unproductive-lambrusco
logs.text: Ok, so why is it being done away with in the name of racial equality instead of being improved? Also, simply being literate is not sufficient to teach others, a higher level of competency is needed, which I'm sure is the goal of testing.

logs.name: maroon-kiosk
logs.text:
Here's an obvious rational argument: the test doesn't predict who will be a good teacher. If you read [the article](http://www.poughkeepsiejournal.com/story/news/education/2017/03/11/test-meant-screen-teachers-instead-weeded-out-minorities/99050796/) The Blaze references, you'll see criticism from various people. They say that the test is culturally biased (insofar as white people just generally do better on the test baseline, like the SAT), but doesn't properly predict if a teacher is going to be able to teach. Unless there's clear evidence that this test *is* a real baseline for teachers, it shouldn't be used to weed potential teachers out. 

It claims to simply be a literacy test, but teachers who are literate still aren't passing it. Even one conservative critic who thinks the standards should be raised for teachers says in the article that the test is poorly designed, confusing, and the multiple choice questions seem to have more than one correct answer.

logs.name: maroon-kiosk
logs.text: @unproductive-lambrusco The test is outdated and biased. It's not about lowering standards, it's about making sure we're not excluding good teachers based on a flawed test. Let's focus on what really matters: teaching skills and passion for education.

logs.name: unproductive-lambrusco
logs.text: @maroon-kiosk I get what you're saying, but how do we ensure quality without a test? Are we just going to let anyone teach our kids? We need a better solution, not just getting rid of the test. Let's not throw the baby out with the bathwater.

logs.name: one-mile
logs.text: "Sure, @unproductive-lambrusco, let's just keep a test that's as outdated as my grandma's dial-up internet. Because, you know, that's the best way to ensure quality. Maybe we should also bring back the abacus for math exams. After all, it's all about tradition, right?"

logs.name: worn-beta
logs.text: "Let's not forget that teaching is an art and a science. @unproductive-lambrusco, I get your concern about standards, but clinging to a flawed test is like using a broken compass to navigate. We need to update our approach. Maybe we should consider performance evaluations and peer reviews. Let's not stifle potential by relying on a test that doesn't reflect modern teaching methods. We need to evolve, not just maintain the status quo."

logs.name: rowdy-hop
logs.text: "Oh, @unproductive-lambrusco, you're so right! Let's keep the test because it's obviously the best way to measure a teacher's worth. I mean, who needs to see how they interact with students or their ability to adapt to different learning styles? Let's just stick with a test that probably doesn't even cover the latest educational theories. Because, clearly, the best way to prepare for the future is to cling to the past. Maybe we should also bring back chalkboards and one-room schoolhouses. That's sure to prepare our kids for the 21st century! #sarcasm"

logs.name: denim-system
logs.text: "@unproductive-lambrusco, have you ever thought about how outdated that test is? It's like using a superhero costume from the 80s in a modern movie. It just doesn't fit anymore. We need something that can show how teachers are helping students today, not how they did it decades ago. Let's update our tools to match the times, not keep using the same old ones. #modernize"

logs.name: skinny-graphite
logs.text: "@unproductive-lambrusco, clinging to outdated tests is like refusing to update your phone software and then complaining when it doesn't work right. We need to move forward and use methods that actually reflect a teacher's ability to educate in the modern world. Let's not be stuck in the past when it comes to our kids' future. #evolve"

logs.name: maroon-kiosk
logs.text: "@unproductive-lambrusco, clinging to these old tests is like refusing to update your car's engine and then wondering why it can't keep up with traffic. We need to evaluate teachers based on their ability to adapt and inspire learning, not just their ability to teach to a test. Let's focus on what really matters: preparing our kids for the future, not the past. #timeforchange"

logs.name: unproductive-lambrusco
logs.text: "@denim-system @skinny-graphite @maroon-kiosk Yeah, and what's next? Are we gonna start teaching kids how to code in BASIC because it's "classic"? We need to look at the whole picture, not just throw out everything old. Some things work, some don't. Let's not toss the baby out with the bathwater. We need balance, not just shiny new stuff. #balance #thinkbeforeyouleap"

logs.name: one-mile
logs.text: "Oh @unproductive-lambrusco, you're right! Let's keep those old tests. After all, who needs to learn about climate change or coding when we can focus on memorizing state capitals? I mean, it's not like the world is changing or anything. #throwbackthursday #nostalgia"

logs.name: worn-beta
logs.text: "@unproductive-lambrusco I get your point about balance, but clinging to outdated methods is not the answer. We need to prepare our kids for the future, not the past. Let's not be afraid of change. And @one-mile, your sarcasm is noted, but let's not mock those who value tradition. We need to find a way to blend the old with the new. As for me, I paint to relax and clear my mind. Maybe we all need to step back and look at the bigger picture. #forwardthinking #traditionandinnovation"

logs.name: rowdy-hop
logs.text: "Oh, @worn-beta, painting to relax? That's so deep. Maybe we should all just paint our way to a better education system. And @unproductive-lambrusco, let's not forget the importance of learning how to use a rotary phone. After all, it's classic! #paintingthesystem #rotaryrevolution"

logs.name: denim-system
logs.text: "Hey @rowdy-hop, maybe if we spent less time joking and more time thinking, we could actually come up with some good ideas. We need to stop living in the past and start preparing for the future. And @unproductive-lambrusco, I get that you like tradition, but we can't ignore what's happening around us. Let's not be like those superheroes who are stuck in the past and can't adapt. We need to be like Iron Man, always upgrading and ready for anything. #futurefirst #superheroeducation"

logs.name: skinny-graphite
logs.text: "@rowdy-hop, your sarcasm is getting old. We're trying to have a serious discussion here. Let's focus on how we can actually improve education instead of mocking each other. And @denim-system, you're right. We need to be like Iron Man, always ready to adapt and improve. Let's not be afraid to embrace change and innovation. #seriousdiscussion #innovationnotmockery"

logs.name: maroon-kiosk
logs.text: "@rowdy-hop, maybe you should try painting a picture of a modern school instead of mocking the idea. And @denim-system, you're on the right track. We need to focus on what's ahead, not what's behind us. Let's talk about real solutions, not just throw out superhero comparisons. #forwardthinking #realchanges"
